expectations.text: States the lex specialis principle: under NIS2 Art. 4, sector-specific Union legal acts with at-least-equivalent effect take precedence, and DORA (Regulation (EU) 2022/2554) is that act for financial entities including credit institutions
expectations.evidence: 'DORA (Regulation (EU) 2022/2554) operates as lex specialis relative to NIS2 under NIS2's own Art. 4: for financial entities, DORA's ICT risk-management and incident-reporting rules apply instead of NIS2 Arts. 21 and 23.' Full mechanism explained under 'The legal mechanism: lex specialis under NIS2 Art. 4'.

expectations.text: Specifies WHICH provisions are displaced: DORA applies instead of NIS2 Art. 21 (cybersecurity risk-management measures) and Art. 23 (incident reporting) for banks
expectations.evidence: 'DORA replaces NIS2 Art. 21 (risk management measures)...' and 'DORA replaces NIS2 Art. 23 (incident reporting)...' stated explicitly.

expectations.text: Notes what remains relevant from NIS2 despite DORA precedence (e.g., banks remain listed entities / national registration and information-sharing frameworks may still apply; supervision of ICT risk sits with financial authorities under DORA)
expectations.evidence: 'What NIS2 still does apply to you... Registered/listed as a NIS2 entity under your Member State's transposition — banks stay on the NIS2 register... Subject to the general NIS2 framework provisions... (jurisdictional/scope provisions, cooperation mechanisms).'

expectations.text: Recommends a single integrated programme built on DORA's requirements (ICT risk management framework, incident classification and reporting, digital operational resilience testing including TLPT, ICT third-party risk, information sharing) mapped against NIS2 for residual obligations — not two separate programmes
expectations.evidence: 'No — build one programme to DORA, with a light NIS2 registration/coordination layer, not two duplicate substantive programmes.' The table maps ICT risk management, third-party risk, resilience testing (incl. TLPT), and incident reporting to DORA chapters, with registration/governance mapped to residual NIS2 items.

expectations.text: Any incident-reporting specifics given match DORA's regime (major ICT-related incidents reported to the financial competent authority with initial/intermediate/final reports) rather than NIS2 CSIRT reporting
expectations.evidence: 'incident reports go to your financial supervisor (e.g., national competent authority under DORA, potentially the ECB/EBA framework...), not to the national CSIRT. Do not build a parallel CSIRT notification track for the same ICT incidents.'
